title: Moroccan Seffa with Egg
description: A traditional Moroccan dish made with wheat semolina and egg, perfect for a quick and easy breakfast or brunch.
instructions: In a large saucepan, bring 4 cups of water to a boil.
Add salt and 2 tablespoons of mounded.
Add 1 cup of seffa and stir well.
Reduce heat to low and cook for 10-12 minutes or until the semolina is cooked and the water is absorbed.
In a small bowl, beat the egg.
Add 1 teaspoon of on and 1/4 teaspoon of metal to the egg and mix well.
Pour the egg mixture over the cooked semolina and stir well.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the egg is set.
Serve hot and garnish with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
